front: How does the AWS Glue Data Catalog work as an Iceberg catalog?
back: AWS Glue Data Catalog is a managed metadata store that Iceberg can use as its catalog via the GlueCatalog implementation. Tables registered in Glue are discoverable by Athena, Glue ETL jobs, EMR, and other AWS services. Configuration for Spark on EMR: set `spark.sql.catalog.glue_catalog = org.apache.iceberg.spark.SparkCatalog`, `spark.sql.catalog.glue_catalog.catalog-impl = org.apache.iceberg.aws.glue.GlueCatalog`, and `spark.sql.catalog.glue_catalog.warehouse = s3://my-bucket/warehouse`. Iceberg uses Glue's table properties to store the current metadata location pointer, with optimistic locking via Glue's conditional update API to prevent concurrent writer conflicts. Athena v3 supports Iceberg natively — you can query Glue-registered Iceberg tables directly in the Athena console without additional configuration. This stack (Flink/Spark → Iceberg → S3 → Glue catalog → Athena) is the canonical AWS open lakehouse architecture and increasingly the default for teams building new data platforms on AWS.
